revision.text:
'''山畑古墳群'''（やまはたこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[東大阪市]]南東部（上四条町、瓢箪山町あたり）に分布する[[群集墳]]。東大阪市内では最大規模の[[古墳群]]。 == 概要 == {{出典の明記| section = 1| date = 2022-10}} 6世紀後半から7世紀はじめにかけて築造され、大半は直径10～15m程度の[[円墳]]であるが、双円墳や上円下方墳も含まれる。かつては100基以上あったとされるが、住宅開発などで破壊されたものも含めて70基ほどが確認されており、大半は[[東大阪市立郷土博物館]]の近辺周囲に密集している。基本的に横穴石室を持ち、副葬品として馬具類が多くの古墳から発見されている。この辺りは騎馬を飼育していた[[馬飼部]]を率いた渡来系氏族の勢力地（いわゆる「馬飼いの里」）とされ、これらの墳墓はその一族のものと推定される。 == 主な古墳 == {{出典の明記| section = 1| date = 2022-10}} ; 山畑22号墳 : 郷土博物館敷地内にある。東西に円丘をもつ双円墳だったが現在は東丘は消失しており、直径15m、墳丘高約4.5mの西丘と横穴石室が保存されている。発掘調査で石室から土器・装身具・馬具等が出土し、博物館にて保管・展示されている。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|scale:20000}} : なお、郷土博物館敷地内には22号墳の他に38,39,40,41号墳があるが、すでに墳丘は無く、石室内部が露出（あるいは全損）している。 ; 山畑51号墳 : 通称「成山古墳」。直径約15m、墳丘高約3mの円墳で、石室の開口はない。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|scale:20000}} ; 山畑52号墳 : 山畑古墳群の中でいちばん標高の低い位置にある。6世紀後半頃の南北の墳丘長が約50mの双円墳と推定され、その形状から通称「'''瓢箪山'''」と呼ばれる。北側は「大塚」、南側は「鬼塚」と呼ばれる。 : 西側に[[瓢箪山稲荷神社]]がある。かつて北丘（大塚）の石室内で狐が飼われていた。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|scale:20000}} == ギャラリー == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|2号墳 File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|2号墳 石室 File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|22号墳
{{small|双円墳であったが右奥の東墳丘は破壊。}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|22号墳 石室 File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|39号墳 File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|40号墳 File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|52号墳 == 関連項目 == * [[瓢箪山稲荷神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}}{{脚注の不足|section=1|date=2022-10}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html 郷土博物館と山畑古墳群：東大阪市タウン情報] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html 博物館へようこそ]（東大阪市立郷土博物館公式サイト） ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html -山畑古墳群 その1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html -山畑古墳群 その2 -] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまはたこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[山畑古墳群]] to [[Yamahata Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

'''山畑古墳群'''（やまはたこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[東大阪市]]南東部（上四条町、瓢箪山町あたり）に分布する[[群集墳]]。東大阪市内では最大規模の[[古墳群]]。 == 概要 == {{出典の明記| section = 1| date = 2022-10}} 6世紀後半から7世紀はじめにかけて築造され、大半は直径10～15m程度の[[円墳]]であるが、双円墳や上円下方墳も含まれる。かつては100基以上あったとされるが、住宅開発などで破壊されたものも含めて70基ほどが確認されており、大半は[[東大阪市立郷土博物館]]の近辺周囲に密集している。基本的に横穴石室を持ち、副葬品として馬具類が多くの古墳から発見されている。この辺りは騎馬を飼育していた[[馬飼部]]を率いた渡来系氏族の勢力地（いわゆる「馬飼いの里」）とされ、これらの墳墓はその一族のものと推定される。 == 主な古墳 == {{出典の明記| section = 1| date = 2022-10}} ; 山畑22号墳 : 郷土博物館敷地内にある。東西に円丘をもつ双円墳だったが現在は東丘は消失しており、直径15m、墳丘高約4.5mの西丘と横穴石室が保存されている。発掘調査で石室から土器・装身具・馬具等が出土し、博物館にて保管・展示されている。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|scale:20000}} : なお、郷土博物館敷地内には22号墳の他に38,39,40,41号墳があるが、すでに墳丘は無く、石室内部が露出（あるいは全損）している。 ; 山畑51号墳 : 通称「成山古墳」。直径約15m、墳丘高約3mの円墳で、石室の開口はない。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|scale:20000}} ; 山畑52号墳 : 山畑古墳群の中でいちばん標高の低い位置にある。6世紀後半頃の南北の墳丘長が約50mの双円墳と推定され、その形状から通称「'''瓢箪山'''」と呼ばれる。北側は「大塚」、南側は「鬼塚」と呼ばれる。 : 西側に[[瓢箪山稲荷神社]]がある。かつて北丘（大塚）の石室内で狐が飼われていた。 : {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|scale:20000}} == ギャラリー == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|2号墳 File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|2号墳 石室 File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|22号墳
{{small|双円墳であったが右奥の東墳丘は破壊。}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|22号墳 石室 File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|39号墳 File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|40号墳 File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|52号墳 == 関連項目 == * [[瓢箪山稲荷神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}}{{脚注の不足|section=1|date=2022-10}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html 郷土博物館と山畑古墳群：東大阪市タウン情報] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html 博物館へようこそ]（東大阪市立郷土博物館公式サイト） ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html -山畑古墳群 その1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html -山畑古墳群 その2 -] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまはたこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]] [[Category:群集墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]]

Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka, Osaka|ja|東大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]]

The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:東大阪市の歴史
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]]

Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Higashiosaka
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:群集墳]]

Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:群集墳]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}}

It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Clustered Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:群集墳]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiōsaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:群集墳]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:群集墳]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image = | affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). {{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Yamahata Kofun Group]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}}

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|qid=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}}
