Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|qq=|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11470760}}

The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11470760}}

It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470760]] Wikidata name: Yamahata Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location =[[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] – Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}.
