The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ([[:en:Toraijin|Toraijin]]) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location ={{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} – Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|WD=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location ={{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} – Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|WD=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location ={{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} – Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|WD=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamahata Kofun Group]][[ja:山畑古墳群]] [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yamahata Kofun Group | image =; File:Yamahata-No.22 Kofun.JPG| affiliation = {{ill|Yamahata Kofun Group|ja|山畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Kofun Group|qq=}} | location ={{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan; {{ill|Higashiosaka|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}| coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} | area = Various | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} – Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | burial goods = Horse fittings, earthenware, burial accessories | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Yamahata Kofun Group''' (山畑古墳群, ''Yamahata Kofun-gun'') is a {{ill|Clustered Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} located in the southeastern part of {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka, Osaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan (around Kamishijō-chō and Hyōtan-yama-chō). It is the largest kofun cluster in Higashiōsaka City. == Overview == The kofun were built from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. Most of them are small circular tombs ({{ill|Empun (Round Kofun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun (Round Kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) with diameters of 10–15 meters, but there are also twin circular tombs and unique types like a top-round, bottom-square tomb ({{ill|Jōen Kahōfun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen Kahōfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Joenkahofun|WD=Q11358496}}). Originally, over 100 tumuli were believed to exist, but due to urban development, about 70 have been confirmed. Most are concentrated around the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}. The kofun typically feature corridor-style stone chambers ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja|横穴石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and numerous burial goods related to horse gear have been excavated. The surrounding area is thought to have been a settlement of an immigrant clan ({{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|WD=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}}) who managed horse breeding and cavalry ({{ill|Umakaibe|ja|馬飼部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umakaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), leading to the nickname "Village of Horse Breeders." The kofun are believed to be the burial sites of this influential group. == Major Kofun == === Yamahata No. 22 Kofun === Located within the grounds of the {{ill|Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|ja|東大阪市立郷土博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum|1=Higashiōsaka Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654285}}, it was originally a twin circular kofun ({{ill|Sōenfun|ja|双円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōenfun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) with eastern and western mounds. However, the eastern mound has been destroyed. The remaining western mound has a diameter of 15 meters and a height of approximately 4.5 meters, with a preserved stone chamber. Excavations have yielded earthenware, accessories, and horse fittings, which are housed and displayed in the museum. * {{Coord|34|39|38|N|135|39|04|E|map_size:20000}} * The museum grounds also contain Nos. 38, 39, 40, and 41 kofun, but their mounds have been lost, leaving only exposed or completely destroyed stone chambers. === Yamahata No. 51 Kofun === Also known as "Nariyama Kofun," it is a circular mound with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of 3 meters. The burial chamber is not currently open. * {{Coord|34|39|40|N|135|38|38.5|E|map_size:20000}} === Yamahata No. 52 Kofun === The lowest-altitude kofun in the Yamahata Kofun Group, estimated to be a twin circular mound from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, with a total length of about 50 meters. Due to its type, it is commonly called "'''Hyōtan-yama'''" ("Gourd Mountain"). The northern mound is referred to as "Ōtsuka" (Great Mound), while the southern mound is called "Onizuka" (Demon Mound). * Located near {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}}, where foxes were once kept inside the stone chamber of the northern mound (Ōtsuka). * {{Coord|34|39|39|N|135|38|32|E|map_size:20000}} == Gallery == File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, entrance.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Yamahata Kofun Group-2, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 2 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, funkyu.jpg|No. 22 Kofun
{{small|Originally a twin circular kofun, but the eastern mound (back right) was destroyed.}} File:Yamahata Kofun Group-22, genshitsu-1.jpg|No. 22 Kofun stone chamber File:Yamahata-No.39 Kofun.JPG|No. 39 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.40 Kofun.JPG|No. 40 Kofun File:Yamahata-No.52 Kofun01.JPG|No. 52 Kofun == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|ja|瓢箪山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōtan-yama Inari Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamahata Kofun Group}} * [http://www.hi-osaka.com/kofun.html Folk Museum and Yamahata Kofun Group: Higashiōsaka City Town Intypeation] * [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/index.html Welcome to the Museum] (Higashiōsaka City Folk Museum Official Site) ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub20.siyuhen.yamahaya01.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 1 -] ** [http://www.bunkazaishisetsu.or.jp/k_hakubutsukan/sub22.siyuhen.yamahaya02.html - Yamahata Kofun Group Part 2 -] {{translated page|ja|山畑古墳群|version=91891794|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山畑古墳群]] {{Commons category|Yamahata Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11470760}} [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Higashiosaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
